roleModeNotes: Public data-role values and seam-* role classes identify meaning for CSS, JavaScript, audits, Bricks, and AI tools. They do not apply starter card/button/modal visuals, padding, background, border, shadow, or radius. Build appearance with neutral Seam primitives, universal Kiwe/Seam tokens, and site CSS.

appShellAdoption.purpose: Defines which public Seam classes Kiwe DSA itself may apply inside live sheets/screens. Normal WordPress pages and Bricks layouts may use the full public Seam vocabulary; this map is specifically for Kiwe AppShell internals where generic classes can accidentally alter geometry.

levels.public-adopted: Runtime may add the public seam-* class to existing DSA markup because visual impact is low and reviewed.
levels.shadow-only: Runtime annotates the role with protected data-seam-* metadata only. This preserves AppShell isolation from site CSS even when core Seam role classes are headless.
levels.authority-only: Runtime/state authority only. Themes and Seam must not create behavior for this concern.

eyebrow.reason: Text-only role; existing DSA selectors already own surface typography and color.

caption.reason: Text-only helper; safe for muted supporting copy.

price.reason: Numeric/text-only helper; tabular numeric styling is compatible with DSA price landmarks.

shadowOnly.card: AppShell keeps card semantics on protected data-seam-* metadata so site CSS cannot accidentally style live DSA internals.
shadowOnly.button: AppShell keeps button semantics protected so site CSS cannot override DSA controls.
shadowOnly.input: AppShell keeps field/input semantics protected because Profile, PhoneKey, checkout, and validation states are authority-owned.
shadowOnly.media: AppShell keeps media semantics protected because DSA media geometry is screen-specific.
shadowOnly.badge: AppShell keeps badge semantics protected so site CSS cannot alter notification/status badges.
shadowOnly.nav: AppShell keeps nav semantics protected because dock and context navigation use Geometry Engine variables.
shadowOnly.actions: AppShell keeps action-row semantics protected because action placement is panel/dock/context-owned.
shadowOnly.form: AppShell keeps form semantics protected because Profile, PhoneKey, checkout, and notification forms are authority-owned.
shadowOnly.field: AppShell keeps field semantics protected because validation, account, and checkout fields are authority-owned.
shadowOnly.modal: AppShell keeps modal semantics protected because DSA sheet/screen geometry owns this surface.

protectedShadowAttributes.purpose: Kiwe DSA uses data-seam-* shadow metadata on live AppShell panels so tools can understand framework roles without generic Seam CSS restyling those panels.
protectedShadowAttributes.authorRule: Use public data-role/data-flow/classes for normal page markup. Do not add data-seam-* manually unless writing Kiwe runtime/core integration code.
